entry.title: Re: Can we transport Sustainability control tower object...
entry.content:
Hello Upender,

In your transport landscape, you configure targets that shall receive the to-be-transported content. Based on your description, I assume that the existing route is not used for transporting role collections to SAP Sustainability Control Tower - so, you would have to define a new route with destinations that would point to the right SAP Sustainability Tower endpoints/target accounts. 

If you like, we can have a short call to guide you through the setup process (for this, just drop me a short email at <first name>.<second name>(at)sap.com) - or feel free to open a short incident under component BC-CP-LCM-CAS.

I hope this helps!

Best regards,
Boris Zarske

entry.title: How to migrate to the Commerce Cloud CCv2 (part 2 - media)
entry.content:
Many steps are already given in the CXWorks article Migrate to SAP Commerce Cloud: Migrate Media with azcopy.

This article was republished due to a switch of my account.

Getting a SAS Token

Use the public IP that is for the outgoing Proxy. This will ensure that your IP is always the same, since this the provided IP is in whitelist added for the SAS Token. Other option: check your public IP adress that the server is using by accessing e.g. whatismyip.com or similar service.

However you need to setup the proxy before you use azcopy in the bash (and do not forget to replace username, password, proxyhost and port) .

 

export http_proxy='http://username:password@proxyhost:port/'
export https_proxy='https://username:password@proxyhost:port/'

 

 

As of Nov 2023 is the request of a SAS token done differently than before. The old way is mentioned in the azcopy article.
You need to create a Service request (and not any more a Support ticket)  in SAP for Me - Services and Support. Create a new request and select the option "CRM und Customer Experience" , then select / open Category "CX Commerce Cloud" and select "CX Commerce- Migration Projects" . Select the tile "CX Commerce - Creation of Media SAS token". it is required : environment (e.g. search for *-d1) and add the public IP address you got earlier. if the IP address is wrong, the SAS token is useless.

Not required anymore, but maybe helpful....
The storage id of the hot folder is NOT the same for the media storage and the SAS token will not work.
It might result in a authorization issue. The Media Storage Account Name can be found in the HAC of the cloud system in the Property:  media.globalSettings.cloudAzureBlobStorageStrategy.connection

The value is something like:
DefaultEndpointsProtocol=https;AccountName=xx123xx456xxxx78;AccountKey=XXXXXXXX;EndpointSuffix=core.windows.net

The SAS token is given in the ticket like this containing start and end date (see key st and se) of that token validity. Usually it is valid for 14 days.. You will receive an email with the following details:

======SAS Token List ======
st=2022-11-02T00%3A01Z&se=2022-12-30T00%3A01Z&sp=xxxxxx&sip=1.2.3.4&spr=https&sv=2022-03-28&ss=bqtf&srt=sco&sig=<secretKey>
Storage Name: xx123xx456xxxx78
SecretKey:<One Time Secret>

Please checkout the SecretKey and store it at a save place, since you can view it only one time.
Combine then the path (line one starting with "st=") and exchange the value of the last attribute sig with the secretKey you received.
This together with the host "https://[account-name].blob.core.windows.net/[container]" is needed for the next steps.

Download the azcopy executable from Microsoft to the system where you have the media stored. You do NOT need to have an AD account for this, since you are using a token.
Also : Testing this on a different system will not work, since the outgoing IP address is most likely different.

In case azcopy is not working as expected, you can check the connection and the logs.

Run this PowerShell command to test basic internet connectivity from the host. (Note: you can use any public URL in this test)

 

[System.Net.WebRequest]::Create("http://azcopyvnextrelease.blob.core.windows.net/releasemetadata/latest_version.txt").GetResponse().StatusCode

 

If you run that in PowerShell on the host, one of the following things should happen:

  1. It prints OK. This is the expected result if you run it on your desktop machine.
  2. It fails with an error, that includes the text “(407) Proxy Authentication Required”. Note that this text will be buried inside a larger error message. If this happens, it shows that the traffic is being directed through a proxy that requires authentication. Either the proxy is not used or the current version of AzCopy doesn't work with those proxies.
  3. It fails with some other error. That probably suggests that the host cannot reach the internet. In this case, this is not an AzCopy issue but is a network (configuration) issue.

Using the SAS token

I created an variable (e.g. sas to shorten the links a little, after i checked, if the connection works.

 

export sas="st=2024-05-15T00%3A01Z&se=2024-05-19T00%3A01Z&sp=rwdlacup&sip=193.41.37.3&spr=https&sv=2024-05-19&ss=bqtf&srt=sco&sig=TZADFFz9%2ByIJlLANPi6dQZTuYYiY9gG2qcnfLiY%3D"

 

 

You can use then ${sas} as shown below.
I first sync the sys-master-root and exclude the subfolders (default and also custom ones like "pdfs" or "survey"). Using sync makes it easier when you migrate data with a dry-run.  

Then I move through every folder, however first you need to create the subfolder using 

azcopy make

 

 

./azcopy sync "sys_master" "https://blablabla.blob.core.windows.net/sys-master-root/?${sas}" --recursive=true --delete-destination prompt --exclude-path "backofficeexcelimport;hmc;survey;backofficeconfiguration;pdfs;jasperreports;email-body;impex;catalogsync;images;cronjob"
./azcopy sync "sys_master/images" "https://blablabla.blob.core.windows.net/sys-master-images/images?${sas}" --recursive=true --delete-destination prompt --log-level ERROR
./azcopy sync "sys_master/impex" "https://blablabla.blob.core.windows.net/sys-master-impex/impex?${sas}" --recursive=true --log-level ERROR --delete-destination prompt
./azcopy sync "sys_master/cronjob" "https://blablabla.blob.core.windows.net/sys-master-cronjob/cronjob?${sas}" --recursive=true --log-level ERROR --delete-destination prompt
./azcopy sync "sys_master/catalogsync" "https://blablabla.blob.core.windows.net/sys-master-catalogsync/catalogsync?${sas}" --recursive=true --log-level ERROR --delete-destination prompt
./azcopy sync "sys_master/jasperreports" "https://blablabla.blob.core.windows.net/sys-master-jasperreports/jasperreports?${sas}" --recursive=true --log-level ERROR --delete-destination prompt
./azcopy sync "sys_master/survey" "https://blablabla.blob.core.windows.net/sys-master-survey/survey?${sas}" --recursive=true --log-level ERROR --delete-destination prompt
./azcopy sync "sys_master/pdfs" "https://blablabla.blob.core.windows.net/sys-master-pdfs/pdfs?${sas}" --recursive=true --log-level=ERROR --delete-destination prompt

entry.title: Create Report in SAP by combining SAP Standard Table. Tcode: SQVI
entry.content:
SAP offers an efficient way to create reports by combining tables without needing additional enhancements. This tutorial will demonstrate how to generate a Purchase Order Report by linking two key tables, EKKO (header data) and EKPO (item data), using the SQVI tool.

 

Step 1: Create Sales Order With 2 Items

  • Open the ME21N transaction.
  • Create 2 items

Step 2: Access SQVI (QuickViewer)

  • Open the SQVI transaction.
  • Enter a report name and select the Create option.

Step 3: Set Up Report Configuration

  • Provide a report title.
  • Choose Table Join as the method for linking tables.
  • Select Basis Mode for the report layout.

Step 3: Add Tables and Define Links

  1. Use the Insert Table option to add the required tables (EKKO and EKPO).
  2. The system will automatically identify and link common fields between the tables, such as PO Number and Item Number.

*Make sure the join is correct, in this case you want to display all items from one purchase order. You must delete the connection between LPONR - EBELP by right clicking and choose delete link.

Tip:

  • If additional links are needed, you can manually drag and drop fields between tables. Ensure that the tables you are joining have at least one common technical field

Step 4: Select Output and Input Fields

Return to the main screen and choose the fields for both input and output.

  • Output Fields: These will be displayed on the report's output screen. In this example, we want to know what number of the PO Document with the list of the material that is created based on PO Number.

  • Selection Fields: These will appear as input parameters where you define criteria before running the report. In this example, we want to display based on date.

Step 4: Execute

  • Click Execute

  • Input date

  • Click Execute

Created Report will be same as Created Purchase order

entry.title: Routing: Alternative Sequence & Parallel Sequence in SAP
entry.content:
When creating routing in SAP, there are 3 different types of sequences that you can create:

    • Standard Sequence

      A standard sequence is the sequence of operations that describes a production process and from which parallel and alternative sequences can branch. 

      When we create a routing, the system will automatically create a sequence, the standard sequence. The other sequence is either an alternative or a parallel sequence, but they will be connected with the standard routing. Alternative sequences are only for routings, but parallel sequences can be used for routings and rate routings.

    • Alternative Sequence

      An alternative sequence is a sequence of operations that can be used as an alternative to a number of consecutive operations from the standard sequence. This enables you to produce a product in different ways.

      Alternative sequences are used, for example, if the production process differs according to lot size, or if there is a downtime. This is also useful when there is capacity overload, and resource or machine breakdown.

      In creating a new routing sequence, you can go to routing CA01 and choose the Sequences icon.



      Choose the new entry icon to select which sequence you want to create.

      In the sequence details, decide the reference data by entering the branch operation and return operation. 

      Branch operation is from which an alternative sequence is to replace the reference sequence. This is the point in the routing where the system leaves the main sequence and enters an alternative sequence. It serves as the decision point to switch from the standard sequence to an alternative sequence.

       

      Return operation is the operation where the system exits the alternative sequence and returns to the main sequence. After the alternative sequence is completed, production resumes from the return operation, ensuring continuity in the production process.

      Meanwhile the alternative sequence fields are filled with the range of lot size of the production. For Example, the lot sizes are from 200 to 500. So, the system will automatically trigger to use the alternative sequence if the production order lot size is 200 or more until 500.

      • Parallel Sequence

        A parallel sequence enables you to process several operations at the same time and is carried out as the corresponding section in the standard sequence. These are special types of overlapping operations or if several operations are processed simultaneously.  This is the case in the process industry.

        This is useful for minimizing lead times, increasing output, and improving utilization. For example, when we handle complex products, when there is high volume production, also important if there’s no dependency between 2 operations.

        But there are considerations of using parallel sequences which will be complex in planning, monitoring, and resource availability.

        To create a parallel sequence, choose the parallel sequence when creating a new entry.
        In the sequence details, decide the reference data by entering the branch operation and return operation. For example:

        Branch operation is set for operation 0060 which is visual inspection. Then, the Return operation is set for operation 0070 which is labelling. This means all operations from 0060 to 0070 are running at the same time or processed simultaneously.

        So that’s basically the way you create a new routing sequence. This sequence will impact on the production order creation after the MRP runs are created. The difference of the sequence will determine which workstation or resource will be used in the production execution.

        Cheers,
        Dafina

entry.title: Returnable Transport Packaging (RTP) in SAP S4/HANA: Business Process, Manuals & Configuration
entry.content:
Basic Concept

  • Packaging
    • Definition

The term packaging encompasses three kinds: 

Packaging Type

Description

Primary Packaging

Directly protect the product.

Secondary Packaging

Holds multiple primary packages to ease counting & handling (e.g., boxes, containers).

Tertiary Packaging

Large-scale storage and transport (e.g., pallets, shipping containers).

  1. Packaging Owner

The owner of the packaging can be either:

Packaging Owner

Supplier

Customer

Service Provider (The logistics)

  • Material Type for packaging scenario

Material Type

Explanation

LEIH

Only quantity is tracked – used in RTP

VERP

Both value & quantity are tracked

  • Returnable Packaging Scenario Supported by SAP

Scenario

Scope

Description

Stock Ownership

Returnable Packaging to Vendor (RTP)

Inventory Management

Manages vendor-owned returnable packaging stored on-site as special stock.

Vendor

Returnable Packaging Processing (RPP)

Sales

Manages company-owned returnable packaging at customer locations. Customers pay if not returned.

Company

Returnable Packaging Logistics (RPL)

Automotive

Tracks returnable packaging in automotive supply chains. OEMs provide empty packaging to suppliers, who return it filled.

OEM

SAP Returnable Packaging Management

Industry Cloud

Integrates RTP & RPP, tracking returnable asset across industries

Varies

 

In this article, we will cover the RTP (Returnable Packaging to Vendor) Scenario.

  • Example Industry using RTP

  • Business Process

 

For RTP, the process flow in the business is categorized into three categories,

  • For supplier-owned packaging

Supplier sends materials with packaging → Packaging is received by the company (customer) along with the material → Material is unpacked and used, while the packaging is stored → Empty packaging is returned to the supplier for reuse → Tracking of packaging material is done via returnable packaging accounts.

This is the scenario that we want to demonstrate in this article.

  • For customer-owned packaging

Company (customer) sends empty packaging to the supplier → Supplier delivers materials using the provided packaging → Material is unpacked and used, and the packaging remains with the company → Packaging is refilled/reused for subsequent shipments → Ownership of the packaging remains with the customer throughout the process

  • For service provider-owned packaging

A third-party service provider supplies empty packaging to the supplier → Supplier sends materials using service provider's packaging → Material is received and unpacked by the company →Empty packaging is collected by the service provider from the company → Service provider manages the cycle, including cleaning, maintenance, and redistribution to the supplier.

  • SAP Demonstration
    • Process Flow

      Two of many ways to demonstrate RTP in the SAP System:

      • The RTP goods can be created while doing GR against PO and adding new line using Transport equipment (mvt 501 M).
      • In some cases vendor demands to include the RTP goods in original Purchase Order.

Here is the SAP process flow for the first scenario:

Step 1 - Create material with material type LEIH

T-CodeMM01

Fill out the material type as well as material group as returnable packaging.

Step 2 - Create Purchase Order for the main material (the material wrapped in the packaging)

T-CodeME21N

Step 3 - Goods Receipt Material, Add Transport Equipment

T-CodeMIGO - 501M

Stock Checking

T-CodeMMBE

Step 4 - Return the packaging material to vendor.

T-CodeMIGO - 502M

 

Summary of RTP in SAP S/4HANA

Returnable Transport Packaging (RTP) is supplier-owned, non-valuated special stock used across industries to reduce costs and waste. In SAP, RTP is automatically tracked via special stock type ‘M’, ensuring seamless procurement, goods movement, and returns.

entry.title: Live Sessions im SAP Learning Hub: Interaktiv SAP lernen
entry.content:
Was sind Live Sessions im SAP Learning Hub?

Live Sessions sind Online-Veranstaltungen, die in der Regel ein bis zwei Stunden dauern und von SAP-Experten geleitet werden. Sie finden das ganze Jahr über statt und decken ein breites Spektrum an SAP-Themen ab. Im Jahr 2024 haben über 200.000 Lernende dieses Angebot genutzt. Um möglichst viele Teilnehmende weltweit zu erreichen, werden die Sessions in zwei Zeitzonen und in englischer Sprache angeboten.

Die Live Sessions haben verschiedene Formate. Sie sind zu einem Großteil

  • Expert Deep Dives zu bestimmten SAP Themen.

Darüber hinaus gibt es

  • What’s New Sessions,
  • Stay Certified Sessions und
  • Get Started Sessions.

Gerade die Deep Dives und die Get Started Sessions haben eine auf Interaktion und Kollaboration ausgerichtetes Set up, das eine gute Interaktion zwischen Lernenden und Experten ermöglicht.

Vorteile der Live-Sessions im SAP Learning Hub

Diese Live-Sessions bieten eine Vielzahl von Vorteilen:

  1. Expertenwissen: Aktuelles Expertenwissen: SAP-Fachleute präsentieren die neuesten Themen und Technologien. Dies gewährleistet, dass Teilnehmer stets über die aktuellsten Entwicklungen und Produkte informiert sind.
  2. Interaktivität: Teilnehmer können in Echtzeit Fragen stellen und Diskussionen führen. Dies fördert nicht nur das Verständnis in komplexe Sachverhalte, sondern ermöglicht auch den Austausch von Erfahrungen und Best Practices. Auch können sie von Fragen anderer lernen.
  3. Praxisorientierung: Die Sessions konzentrieren sich oft auf reale Anwendungsfälle. Dies hilft den Teilnehmenden, das Gelernte direkt in ihrem Arbeitsalltag umzusetzen.
  4. Networking: Teilnehmer haben die Möglichkeit, sich mit Gleichgesinnten aus der SAP-Community auszutauschen. Dies fördert den Wissensaustausch und kann zu wertvollen beruflichen Kontakten führen.
  5. Flexibilität: Die Sessions werden zu verschiedenen Zeiten angeboten, um unterschiedliche Zeitzonen und Arbeitszeiten zu berücksichtigen. Zudem sind viele Sessions auch als Aufzeichnung verfügbar.
  6. Kostengünstigkeit: Für Abonnenten des SAP Learning Hub sind die Live Sessions im Abonnement enthalten, was eine kosteneffiziente Möglichkeit zur kontinuierlichen Weiterbildung darstellt.
  7. Aktualität der Themen: Die Themen der Live Sessions spiegeln die aktuellen Trends und Entwicklungen in der SAP-Welt wider. In jüngster Zeit gab es beispielsweise Sessions zu: SAP S/4HANA Cloud - SAP Business Technology Platform - SAP Analytics Cloud - Integration verschiedener SAP-Lösungen und viele Sessions zu Artificial Intelligence.

Die Live Sessions im SAP Learning Hub ergänzen die on-demand Lerninhalte optimal und bieten SAP-Profis die Möglichkeit, ihr Wissen kontinuierlich zu erweitern und auf dem neuesten Stand zu bleiben. Sie sind ein wichtiger Baustein für die professionelle Entwicklung in der sich schnell wandelnden Welt der SAP-Technologien.

Für Interessierte empfiehlt es sich, regelmäßig das Angebot im SAP Learning Hub zu prüfen und relevante Live Sessions in die persönliche Weiterbildungsplanung einzubeziehen. Bei der Auswahl der relevanten Live Sessions helfen die Selektionsfilter wie Level (Vorkenntnisse) oder Rolle des Lernenden.

Die Kombination aus flexiblem Online-Lernen und interaktiven Live-Veranstaltungen macht den SAP Learning Hub zu einer umfassenden Ressource für alle, die in der SAP-Welt up-to-date bleiben möchten.

Das Live-Session-Angebot wird unter learning.sap.com veröffentlicht und kontinuierlich auf alle aktuellen Produktbereiche ausgeweitet. Hier finden Sie die die kommenden Live Sessions.

Für regelmäßige Updates zum Lernangebot der SAP empfehlen wir, den SAP Training Newsletter zu abonnieren.

Wir freuen uns über Fragen und Anmerkungen in den Kommentaren.

entry.title: Variable Pay 5 Configuration Files - EC Integrated
entry.content:
5 Important Files of Variable Pay Template Configuration

  1. Business Goals
  2. Employee History File
  3. Bonus Plan Eligibility Rules
  4. Bonus Plans
  5. Business Goal Weights

 

Navigation to the Files Configuration:

Compensation Home --> Select Variable Pay Template --> Manage Plan Details & Manage Users

 

 

  • Business Goals

Configuration File

Significance

Technical Details

Business Goals (.csv)

Contains the business goal definitions, goal forecasts, and goal results.  The variable pay module requires this information to determine the goal-level payouts which impact the bonus calculation.

Upload Variable Pay Objective Template in Provisioning and Map the same in the Variable Pay Plan Template Under Set Bonus Calculation.

 

Provisioning Setup: Upload Variable Pay Objective Plan Template under Managing Plan Template

 

        

 

Sample XML Template –

 

Note – <obj-plan-id>8005</obj-plan-id> will be used while uploading the business goals .csv file.

Instance Setup: Choose the Uploaded Goal Plan Name from the drop-down list.

 

 

File Details: Column F ID should match with the Goal Plan Template ID.

 

 

  • Employee History File

Configuration File

Significance

Technical Details

Employee History File (.csv)

Contains the employee’s date effective history records.  The variable pay module requires this information to determine bonus plan eligibility, bonus basis, and proration for each employee

Upload Succession Data Model with "VarPayEmpHistData" Background Element and Map the same in the Variable Pay Plan Template Under Settings.

 

Provisioning Setup: Upload Succession Data Model under Succession Management – Import/Export Data Model.

 

Sample XML Template –

 

 

 

Note – Record Type field must be added for all EC – Integrated Templates

Instance Setup: Choose the Uploaded Background Element from the drop-down list under Settings.

 

In EC Integrated Templates, map the fields to fetch the assignment records from Employee Central to Employee History File.

This Mapping is done under Settings --> Employee Central Employee History Field Mapping

 

 

Eligibility Rule for History Records:

This Eligibility rule will help in pulling the eligible employees assignment records. Here is the sample eligibility rule.

Eligibility Rule is Configured with Scenario – Define Eligibility Rule for Variable Pay Planning and selected under Settings --> Employee Central Settings.

 

Now, Template is ready to fetch the data from EC to EHF by pushing the process button by clearing the checkboxes as below:

 

Note – This can be exported as a .csv file for manually adjustments to assignment records wherever applicable.

 

  • Bonus Plan Eligibility Rule

Configuration File

Significance

Technical Details

Bonus Plan Eligibility Rule

Contains the eligibility rule definitions for the bonus plans

MDF Rule Configuration with Scenario - Define Eligibility Rule for Variable Pay Planning.

 Instance Setup:

This Eligibility rule will help in assigning the bonus plan to the employees.

Eligibility Rule is Configured with Scenario – Define Eligibility for Variable Pay Planning and mentioned in the Bonus Plans file upload.

Once Eligibility Rules are mapped to the Bonus Plans, The List of Eligibility rules can be seen under Manage Plan Details --> Bonus Plan Eligibility Rules

 

 

 

Note – In EC Integrated Templates, Legacy eligibility rules (.csv) file can also be used.

 

  • Bonus Plan

Configuration File

Significance

Bonus Plan (.csv)

Contains the Bonus Plan definition with Split Percentage distribution of Individual, team, and Business Performance Factor

 Instance Setup:

Bonus Plan can be created under Manage Plan Details --> Bonus Plans

 

File Details: Column A is Bonus Plan Name, Column D is the Bonus Eligibility Rule and other columns will have section weights.

 

 

  • Business Goal Weights

Configuration File

Significance

Business Goals Weights

(.csv)

Links the business goals to the bonus plans and assigns unique weights to this relationship.

 

Instance Setup:

This flat file can be imported or exported from Instance under Manage Plan Details --> Import/Export Business Goal Weights

 

 

Later, the weights can be viewed under Bonus Plans --> Open any Bonus Plan and click on Configure Business Goal Weights

 

 

File Details: Column A is Bonus Plan Name, Column B is the Business Goal Name, Column C is the weight (in a scale of 0 to 1) and Column D is in which order to display under business section of worksheet.

 

Conclusion: These 5 Configuration Files are important to push the Calculate Bonus before launching the worksheets.

entry.title: Document Fails to Route to New Manager: Fixing Form Stuck Issues in the PMGM
entry.content:
Problem statement:

Frequent organizational changes and complex third-party integrations can result in inconsistent manager assignments, particularly for Employee Profile (EP) employees. While Employee Central (EC) handles automatic document transfers during manager changes, EP employees often face issues when conditions for automatic transfers aren’t met. This results in incorrect manager assignments, forms not routing properly, forms getting stuck with the former manager, former managers retaining access, and new managers being unable to evaluate performance.

This Excel-based workaround helps identify and fix incorrect manager assignments, allowing quick rerouting of forms to the correct manager. While SAP recommends "rocking" forms, SuccessFactors lacks a built-in solution for detecting false managers, especially during large organizational changes. The process is simple for anyone with basic Excel skills and can be enhanced with macros or automation tools, providing a practical solution for PMGM consultants during frequent restructuring.

The screenshot is from the KBA that provides details on 'Forms Not Transferring to New Manager' or 'Failed to Route to Manager.

Workaround:

Step 1: Create and Download Predefined Report from SuccessFactors Reporting Module

  • Generate the predefined report within the SuccessFactors Reporting Module and download it for further analysis
  • Navigate to the SuccessFactors Reporting module.

  • Create a Cross-Domain Table Report: Create a cross-domain table report using the Performance Management and Employee Profile (EP) domains. The EP domain identifies new manager/Matrix Manager changes, while the Performance Management domain shows the old manager to whom the form is stuck.

  • In the "People" tab, choose "Other Filters."

  • Select necessary columns: Choose the required columns for the workaround. The essential columns are listed below, but additional columns can be included based on specific requirements.

  • Apply filters to narrow down results: Under the 'Filters' tab, apply the following filters:
    • Set the form status to "Modify Stage."
    • Filter for "Active" forms only.

Additional filters can be applied as needed. Refer to this link for guidance on working with table reports.

Automation idea: This report can be automated by scheduling it to run at the desired frequency. The file can then be placed in the SFTP folder. For instructions on how to schedule a report, please refer to this link.

Step 2: Apply Excel formulas and filtering – This can be automated with Macros

a. Compare the current form owner with the manager/matrix manager using True/False formulas in Excel.

Collaborative review process

All steps here are based on the collaborative approach during the modify step. The collaborative review process (C Step) allows multiple roles to participate equally in the review at the same time.

Filter Forms based on Current Owner

The first step is to filter forms based on the current owner during the collaborative step. Forms can be accessed and edited by the employee, their manager, and their matrix manager. To identify and exclude false managers and matrix managers, we will use the following Excel formulas.

  • Open the Excel file downloaded from the report center.
  • Navigate to the Last Populated Cell.
  • Add the Following Columns
    • Add the "Employee Check" column.
    • Add the "Manager Check" column.
    • Add the "Matrix Manager Check" column.

  • Formula for "Employee Check":
    =IF(J2=E2, J2, False)
    • J2: User ID of the employee.
    • E2: User ID of the current owner of the form.
    • If the value in J2 (Employee User ID) matches E2 (Current Owner User ID), the formula will display the value in J2. Otherwise, it will display "False."

  • Formula for "Manager Check":
    =IF(O2=E2, O2, False)
    • O2: User ID of the employee’s manager.
    • E2: User ID of the current owner of the form.
    • If the value in O2 (Manager User ID) matches E2 (Current Owner User ID), the formula will display the value in O2. Otherwise, it will display "False."

  • Formula for "Matrix Manager Check":
    =IF(T2=E2, T2, False)
    • T2: User ID of the employee’s matrix manager.
    • E2: User ID of the current owner of the form.
    • If the value in T2 (Matrix Manager User ID) matches E2 (Current Owner User ID), the formula will display the value in T2. Otherwise, it will display "False."

  • Drag the formulas to all rows in the report

Points to Note:

  • The cell references in the formulas can be adjusted based on the specific Excel report generated.
  • The goal is to compare the current owner of the form with the Employee Profile column details. Since it's a collaborative step, the forms are accessible by all three roles at the same time. These forms will still reflect the old manager/matrix manager details. By using the Employee Profile columns, we can determine the current manager and matrix manager changes.

Based on this comparison, the owners of the form can be confirmed as follows:

    • If AC2 (Employee check cell) contains a user ID, the employee is the owner of the form for that step.
    • Similarly, the owner of the form will be identified using the user IDs in AD2 (Manager check cell) and AE2 (Matrix Manager check cell).
    • If any of these cells show '0', it means the current owner user ID is empty, indicating that no manager or matrix manager is assigned to that employee.

Apply Filters: The "Employee Check" column will always be set to "False," allowing us to identify the population of Managers and Matrix Managers. However, the "Manager Check" and "Matrix Manager Check" columns will still contain data for both True Managers and Matrix Managers, as well as False Managers and Matrix Managers.

  • First, filter the "False" values in the Employee Check, Manager Check, and Matrix Manager Check columns.
  • Filter for active or blank status of Manager
  • This will give us the following:
    1. A population of False Managers and False Matrix Managers.
    2. A False Manager in the Manager Check column could also be a Matrix Manager.
    3. A False Matrix Manager in the Matrix Manager Check column could also be a Manager.

  • Copy Document IDs for the False Population: Copy the Document IDs of the population where all three columns (Employee Check, Manager Check, and Matrix Manager Check) are set to "False" into a separate sheet named "All False".
  • Remove duplicates from the list of Doc Ids

Above steps are essential for routing the forms for huge population to update the new manager/matrix manager details.

b. Identifying Cases with a Single Performance Form Owner and Reassigned Manager

The screenshot below shows that the form owner is only the employee, with the other two fields left blank. After assigning a new manager, the form needs to be "rocked" to reflect the change. To identify document IDs with a single owner, we will use Pivot Tables.

  • Clear Filters in the Main Report
    • Clear any existing filters applied in the main report.
    • Click on the "Select All" button (located at the intersection of row numbers and column letters in the top-left corner of the worksheet).

  • Insert a Pivot Table
    • With all the data selected, insert a Pivot Table into a new sheet. 

Steps in Pivot Table for Manager:

  1. Set Up Pivot Table Fields:
    • Place "Document ID" into the Rows field.
    • Place "Current Owner User ID" into the Values field (set to Count of Current Owner User ID). This will count how many times each owner is assigned to a document.
    • Place "Manager Status" into the Filters field. This allows you to filter and view the forms based on whether the manager is active or inactive.
  2. Filter for Active Managers:
    • In the Pivot Table, set the Manager Status filter to Active. 
  3. Sort Results:
    • Sort the results in the Pivot Table in ascending order (from A to Z). 
  4. Identify and Copy Document IDs:
    • If the Count of Current Owner User ID is 1, copy the corresponding Document IDs into another sheet ‘Pivot Manager’ for further processing.
    • If the Count of Current Owner User ID is not equal to 1(<>1), ignore that entry.

By repeating these steps for the Matrix Manager column, you'll be able to identify forms associated with active-matrix managers and properly route them as needed.

Steps in Pivot Table for Matrix Manager:

  1. Set up Pivot Table Fields:
    • Place "Document ID" into the Rows field.
    • Place "Current Owner User ID" into the Values field (set to Count of Current Owner User ID). This will count how many times each owner is assigned to a document.
    • Place "Matrix Manager Status" into the Filters field. This will allow you to filter and view the forms based on whether the matrix manager is active or inactive.
  2. Filter for Active-Matrix Managers:
    • In the Pivot Table, set the Matrix Manager Status filter to Active. 
  3. Sort Results:
    • Sort the results in the Pivot Table in ascending order (from A to Z). 
  4. Identify and Copy Document IDs:
    • If the Count of Current Owner User ID is 1, copy the corresponding Document IDs into another sheet ‘Pivot matrix manager’ for further processing.
    • If the Count of Current Owner User ID is not equal to 1(<>1), ignore that entry.

Step 3: Final Document IDs uploaded in the Route Form tool

Prepare the final Document IDs:

After identifying the forms with incorrect managers or matrix managers, forms with one Owner gather the Document IDs from the "All False," "Pivot Manager," and "Pivot Matrix Manager" sheets that need routing. These are the forms that require manager reassignment (Please check how to mass route forms).

Upload Document IDs to Route Form Tool:

  • Log in to the SuccessFactors Admin Centre
  • Use the "Route Form" tool to upload the final Document IDs that were identified in the previous steps. 
  • Upload the CSV file containing the document IDs.

Step 4: Rock the form for the changes to reflect

  1. Route the Forms:
    • After uploading the Document IDs into the Route Form tool, initiate the routing process to the correct manager or matrix manager.
  2. Final check:
    • Double-check that all forms have been correctly routed and updated to reflect the new manager and matrix manager assignments.

Additional checks and analysis part:

To identify the inactive managers/matrix managers

Previously, I have only focused on the active or blank status of the manager/matrix manager. For managers with an inactive status, I will identify the appropriate active manager, assign them, and then repeat steps 3 and 4.

    • Clear any existing filters applied in the main report.
    • Filter “Inactive User” in „Manager status “column.
    • Identify the correct manager and route the forms as above(step 3 and 4).
    • Repeat the above steps by filtering “Inactive User” in „Matrix Manager status “column.

Further Analysis in identifying accurately the Wrong Manager/Wrong Matrix Manager:

Above steps 1 through 4 fetches me the document Ids to rock the forms. The following extra steps help determine if an employee has the wrong manager or matrix manager:

Identifying the Wrong Manager:

  • After creating a new sheet with the Document IDs, clear all filters.
  • Reapply filters:
      1. Set the "Employee Check" column to False.
      2. Set the "Manager Check" column to False.
      3. For the "Matrix Manager Check" column, apply a filter to show only rows with numbers (exclude "False" and "0"). This will display the correct Matrix Manager assigned to the form owner.

From these results, you can identify employees assigned with wrong manager, pinpointing where the form is stuck. Copy this population into a separate sheet named "False Manager" for further analysis.

Identifying the Wrong Matrix Manager:

Repeat the previous steps, but change the filter in the "Manager Check" column:

  • After creating a new sheet with the Document IDs, clear all filters.
  • Reapply filters:
      1. Set the "Employee Check" column to False.
      2. In the "Manager Check" column, apply a filter to show only rows with numbers. This will display the correct Manager assigned to the form owner.
      3. Set the "Matrix Manager Check" column to False.

From these results, you can identify employees assigned with wrong matrix manager, showing where the form is stuck. Copy this population into a separate sheet named "False Matrix Manager" for further analysis. 

These steps help narrow down and pinpoint the exact issue with the Manager and Matrix Manager assignment such as if the manager is blank, or incorrect, etc.

The above screenshot can be interpreted as follows: The new manager's change is not reflected in the form as the "current owner user ID" column is blank.

Conclusion:

This proactive approach improved form maintenance by ensuring correct form ownership, preventing missing steps in route maps, and avoiding the creation of unnecessary steps that could cause confusion and permission issues.

Our customer runs this report with Macros and finds it extremely helpful. It saves a significant amount of time, especially when handling multiple requests related to forms stuck due to manager assignments and when needing to route large populations. I hope this workaround will be beneficial for others facing similar challenges.

I would love to hear about your experiences and insights on how you’ve addressed such issues. If you have any suggestions for enhancing or improving this workaround, please feel free to share them. If you have any questions or comments, don’t hesitate to add them below.

Thank you for taking the time to read through this blog! 🙂

entry.title: An Overview at Concur Mobile Security
entry.content:
In this time, where mobility is essential. As more employees work remotely and travel for business, finding secure mobile solutions has never been more critical. SAP Concur offers a robust mobile app that meets the needs of today’s customers. Concur Mobile not only simplifies expense tracking and travel management but also prioritizes security to protect sensitive data. 

Why Mobile Security Matters 

Mobile devices have become a crucial part of business operations, but they also come with unique security challenges. From lost or stolen devices to unsecured networks, mobile environments are highly susceptible to cyber threats. SAP Concur tackles these challenges with a comprehensive suite of security features to safeguard data and ensure compliance. 

Key Features of Concur Mobile Security 

To ensure High-level security, SAP Concur Mobile requires all users to protect their devices with a passcode. The app is designed not to function on jailbroken or rooted devices. If your device is compromised in this way, Concur Mobile will notify you, and you will need to perform a factory reset on your device to continue using the application. This added layer of security helps keep your sensitive information safe. here are some of the key features that SAP Concur app offers:

 1. Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) 

A key element of Concur Mobile security is multi-factor authentication (MFA). By requiring users to provide multiple forms of identification, MFA significantly reduces the risk of unauthorized access. This additional security means that even if login credentials are compromised, they alone are not enough to gain access to sensitive information. 

 2. Biometric Authentication 

Concur Mobile also supports biometric authentication methods such as fingerprint and facial recognition. Biometric authentication adds an extra layer of security by ensuring that only the authorized user can access the app. This method is not only more secure but also more convenient, reducing the need for complex passwords and multiple login steps. Biometric login is turned on by default for SAP Concur Mobile. If you need to make adjustments or customize this feature, simply submit a request to SAP Concur Support 

 3. Data Encryption 

Data encryption is a critical component of Concur Mobile security. Here’s a detailed look at how SAP Concur uses encryption to protect your data: 

 

  • End-to-End Encryption:  SAP Concur uses Transport Layer Security (TLS) and Secure Sockets Layer (SSL) protocols to encrypt data during transmission. This ensures that any data exchanged between the mobile app and SAP Concur's servers is protected from unauthorized interception. 
  • Encryption at Rest: SAP Concur uses Advanced Encryption Standard (AES-256) to encrypt data stored on devices and in the cloud. AES-256 is one of the strongest encryption algorithms available, ensuring that even if a device or server is compromised, the data remains secure. 
  • High Security Account (HSA) :Some customers have high-security requirements that dictate encryption keys must not be stored on the device itself. To meet these needs, company admins can enable the High Security Account (HSA) setting for their accounts. When HSA is enabled, users must create an additional application password that decrypts their data in memory. This extra layer of security means that every time you do a cold start of the app, you'll need to enter this password. It’s an extra step, but it ensures that your data remains protected at all times. 

 4. Single Sign-On (SSO) for Mobile 

Single Sign-On (SSO) is another critical security feature that enhances the user experience while maintaining robust security. SSO allows users to log in once and access multiple applications, reducing the need for multiple logins and passwords. With SAP Concur, SSO is supported on mobile devices, making it easier for employees to manage their travel and expenses securely. 

 5. Mobile Device Management (MDM) Support 

SAP Concur supports Mobile Device Management (MDM) solutions. MDM enables IT administrators to manage and secure mobile devices remotely, ensuring that corporate data is protected at all times. 

 6. Account Lockout Mechanisms  

SAP Concur has implemented a lockout feature for both the web version and the mobile app. If you enter an incorrect username, password, or SSO too many times in a row (as set by your company's administrator), your password or SSO credentials will be cleared. To regain access, you’ll need to follow your company’s specific process for unlocking your account. This lockout mechanism helps protect your account from unauthorized access, ensuring your data remains secure. 

 7. Regular Updates and Patch Management 

Keeping mobile applications up-to-date is crucial for maintaining security. SAP Concur regularly updates the Concur Mobile app to address vulnerabilities and enhance security features. Automatic updates ensure users are always protected by the latest security measures. 

 8. Ensuring Compliance  

SAP Concur Mobile is a key part of the SAP Concur suite and is designed to meet rigorous security standards. These include SAP’s Cloud Services Security Framework, PCI, GDPR, and ISO 27001 certifications. To keep everything secure, the platform goes through annual vulnerability assessments that evaluate both the mobile app and mobile web services. This robust compliance framework ensures your data stays safe and aligns with strict regulatory requirements. 

 9. Integrating Security into Development Practices 

SAP Concur also ensures security at the code level by proactively maintaining up-to-date dependencies and mitigating risks. Automated tools analyze potential vulnerabilities whenever dependencies are updated, ensuring third-party components remain secure. Additionally, static code analysis enforces consistent coding standards while helping to identify and address vulnerabilities in early stages of the development cycle. 

Conclusion

In the present day, having a secure mobile solution is a necessity. SAP Concur’s mobile app is designed to provide businesses with the tools they need to manage travel, expenses, and invoices effectively. By prioritizing mobile security, including robust encryption, biometric authentication, SSO capabilities, MDM support, and comprehensive data protection, SAP Concur helps businesses protect their data while complying with regulations. This makes it an invaluable tool.

entry.title: WalkMe – More than a walk through
entry.content:
It's common knowledge by now that the Concur User Assistant (CUA) is powered by WalkMe and that there are two versions of the CUA - Standard and Custom

Standard content that has been pre-built by SAP Concur that your users can access via Explore Interactive Help from the Help menu or other on-screen prompts.

 

 

Custom built content which has been built specifically for your entity either internally, through a Partner, or with an SAP Concur project team.

A lot of the time it can be viewed that the CUA only provides Smart Walk Throughs (SWT) where users are guided through a particular process through step-by-step on-screen prompts, or with Smart Tips where users can hover over an icon and can view further details.

WalkMe has more potential than this. A lot more.

 

The example that I am going to focus on in this article is one that has come up on a few projects - Users entering credit card expenses as cash expenses.

You know the scenario - a user may not always have the patience to wait for the credit card provider to process the expense, for it to be sent to Concur and then released to the user to add to a claim/report (*). This can lead to a reconciliation irregularity and the need for the accounts/finance team to perform reversals etc. to bring everything back into line.

 (*) I use UK English, where an Expense Report is more commonly known as an Expense Claim

I'll take you through the steps on how you can modify the user’s behavior when building your own custom content.

Step 1

Create a Launcher that will sit on top of the Add Expense button inside a report.

 

 

This will end up becoming an invisible Launcher, so that users will not actually realize they are clicking on it to begin with. But, for the purpose of building and testing it is best left visible so we can see what we are doing with it.

 

Step 2

Create an Automatic Smart Walk Through that will always take the user to the "Available Expenses" tab of the Add Expense to Report popup. What can happen is when a user clicks on the Add Expense button, the pop-up defaults to the last used setting, which can sometimes be the "New Expense" tab; Users then just go on to create the new expense rather than taking a look at what they have available.

 

The aim here will be to mimic Concur's normal behavior - the SWT will need to have two Auto-Steps

  1. Clicking the "Add Expense" button on behalf of the user.
  2. Clicking on the "Available Expenses" tab

 

Step 3

Connect the newly created SWT to the Launcher

 The overall flow will look like this:

User clicks on invisible launcher -> Launcher triggers SWT -> SWT clicks on Add Expense -> SWT clicks on Available Expenses

 

Step 4

Once you are happy with the testing, you can then make the Launcher invisible by selecting Edit on Page:

 

And then selecting the ghost icon:

Don’t forget to Save!

You can, of course, take it a further as the above method doesn't stop uses navigating to the New Expense tab. This is where you can in components to actively warn users against making cash expense entries where they should use what is in their Available Expenses.

Step 5

Here you can employ another invisible Launcher on the New Expense tab.

As before, it remains visible so you can see it for testing and building out the solution.

 

Step 6

This Launcher will kick off another Smart Walk Through - This time however you can start with a popup for the user to warn them.

 

 

Step 7

Add an auto-step to click on the New Expense tab for the users who are making a genuine cash expense entry.

 

As before, once you are happy, don't forget to make the New Expenses Launcher invisible!

 

Every time a user then clicks on, what they believe to be the New Expense tab, it will kick off this SWT and they will receive the warning that you have written.

Of course, the above is just an example - the wording that you can use is very much up to you.

 

Overall, this is just a small sample of what you can achieve with a customized WalkMe solution.

entry.title: EWM Layout-Oriented Storage Control (LOSC) – A simple guide
entry.content:
This post will show you how to set up and test Layout-Oriented Storage Control (LOSC) in SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM). We'll focus on the customization and steps to follow the LOSC process. To start, let's imagine this scenario in an imaginary warehouse:



Step 1: Arrival at the Warehouse (Storage Type 9010)
Goods arrive at the warehouse dock and are unloaded into storage type 9010. They are scanned and entered the EWM system, starting their journey.

Step 2: Quality Inspection (Storage Type 0030)
Next, the goods move to storage type 0030 for quality inspection. Here, each item is checked to ensure it meets the required standards.

Step 3: Intermediate Storage (Storage Type 0035)
After passing the quality check, the goods are transferred to storage type 0035, an intermediate storage area. This is a temporary holding zone.

Step 4: Final Storage (Storage Type 0040)
Finally, the goods reach storage type 0040, the final storage area. Here, products are organized and stored until they are ready to be picked up and shipped to customers.


CUSTOMIZING STEPS

  • SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Master Data -> Define Storage Types

Note: ensure that you have created the activity areas and storage bins for the storage types used in this process. Also, execute the bins sorting.

 

 

 

  • SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Cross-Process Settings -> Warehouse Task -> Define Warehouse Process Type

Note: The WTP for "LOSC" was copied from 1010. Ensure the "Select HU w/o Storage Process" field is checked, as LOSC only works for handling unit warehouse tasks. If unchecked, a product warehouse task will be created, and the process won't work as intended.

 

  • SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Cross-Process Settings -> Warehouse Task -> Define Control Indicators for Determining Warehouse Process Types

 

  • SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Cross-Process Settings -> Warehouse Task -> Determine Warehouse Process Type

 

  • SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Cross-Process Settings -> Warehouse Task -> Define Layout-Oriented Storage Process Control

 Note: In this scenario, we're using the same WPT for LOSC movements. For better traceability, you can create a WPT copied from 3030 to separate the LOSC steps.


TESTING
 

  • Step 1 – Goods receipt

    T-Code: MIGO

Inboud delivery created:

 

  • Step 2 – Inbound Delivery

T-Code: /SCWM/PRDI
Note: WT we’re created automatically using a PPF action but this is a subject to another post.


Note: HU we’re automatically created using packing specification.

 

  • Step 3 – Warehouse Management Monitor

T-Code: /SCWM/MON
Two warehouse tasks (WT) were created because we are using LOSC. The first warehouse task remains with status "B – Waiting":


The second warehouse task uses WPT "3030" to move the handling unit from 9010 to 0030 and has the status "Open":


Confirm the second WT:

A third warehouse task is created automatically when the second is confirmed:


The first WT has the source storage type and bin updated but remains with status "B – Waiting":


The third warehouse task is created to move the HU from 0030 to 0035:

Confirm the third WT:


The first WT has been updated and then the last move of HU is done:


Confirm the first WT:

Process done!


CONCLUSION

Using SAP EWM LOSC helps the warehouse efficiently manage the flow of goods through different storage types. This process ensures products are inspected, stored, and organized in the best way possible, leading to faster order fulfillment and higher customer satisfaction.

I try to make it simple, hope this guide helps you guys.

Best Regards,
Yuri Nogueira
SAP EWM Consultant

entry.title: Role-based requirement approvals in SAP Cloud ALM with external workflows in SAP BPA
entry.content:
Objectives

This SAP blog post gradually outlines how workflows can be used to extend the approval process of requirements by retrieving team roles from an SAP Cloud ALM project. The workflow management system used in this blog post is the SAP Build Process Automation (abbr. SAP BPA) running on SAP BTP.

Prerequisites

  • You have already provisioned an SAP Cloud ALM tenant and SAP Build Process Automation system, which are both running in your SAP BTP system landscape.
  • The connection between SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Build Process Automation was already established via Destinations, Webhooks and Subscriptions to transmit Task API data bidirectionally. If this is not yet done, please review the previous SAP blog post to implement all necessary steps.
  • Additionally, an SMTP mail server destination is configured in your SAP BTP subaccount to receive emails from SAP BPA.
  • A Desktop Agent to execute the automated workflow on SAP BPA is already installed on your local computer.
  • You have been assigned at least the role collections ProcessAutomationAdmin and ProcessAutomationDeveloper in your SAP BTP subaccount.

Procedure Overview

  • Define a custom project team role in SAP Cloud ALM.
  • How the workflow solution design looks like in SAP BPA.
  • Overview about which REST APIs are needed and how they work.
  • How to fetch members of a project role in the subsequent workflow.

Define project team role in SAP Cloud ALM

In the first step, you must go to your SAP Cloud ALM project and add another custom project role which should be called “Requirement Approver”. Following that, this new custom role must be assigned to your project in the Teams tab. Afterwards, assign the corresponding users who should review the requirement later by accepting or rejecting the approval document in SAP BPA. I have assigned two demo users here, however there could be multiple and different approver roles per project.

Workflow Solution design

Let’s focus on the design of the workflow solution in SAP BPA. At the beginning of the process, a trigger is initiated by an event from SAP Cloud ALM that requests the requirement’s data. The outputs of the API trigger are automatically synchronized with the process attributes in SAP BPA. The task details of the requirement are retrieved from SAP Cloud ALM via an HTTP GET method. The task is being identified by its ID via the API connection.

On the level of the second step, the attribute “projectID” which was transmitted via the API from SAP Cloud ALM, must be specified as input variable in the step details. The other two variables “RoleName” and “TeamName” must be manually entered since these aren’t transferred via the API. Those variables will be checked in the subsequent workflow (see next chapter), whether they match.

 

Following this step, the workflow then checks in a branch condition whether the submitted list from the subsequent workflow is empty, in which the users of the project role “Requirement Approver” from SAP Cloud ALM would normally have been listed. Additionally, the branch condition assesses if the requirement status has been set to “In Approval”. Here, the technical name of the requirement status, coming from the API, must be stated.

If one of the two branch conditions hasn’t been met, e.g. if the requirement has the status “Blocked”, the workflow will abort, and an error email will be forwarded to the user who triggered the approval workflow. The approval or rejection of the requirement will be issued via an HTTP POST method to set the status of the task accordingly.

Continuing in the workflow structure, the corresponding approver(s) will receive an email to proceed to the SAP Build Process Automation Inbox. From there they must complete the Requirement Approval document and either approve or reject the requirement manually.

Overview of Rest APIs and their usage

Reading multiple members of a project role from SAP Cloud ALM works by using several REST API interfaces provided from the SAP Business Accelerator Hub. The relevant API are SAP Cloud ALM Tasks and SAP Cloud ALM Projects.

First, the Task API provides the necessary data record which includes the Project ID and is essential to fetch all teams of an SAP Cloud ALM project. This team in SAP Cloud ALM contains multiple project roles, however only the own created “Requirement Approver” project role is relevant. After given the reference from the Task API to the Project ID, all roles of the given PMO team must be finally read out. The assigned users of the project role “Requirement Approver” are listed with their email address in the transmitted Team Roles API.

How to fetch members of a project role

After given this high-level overview about how project roles can be read out with the usage of REST APIs, I would like to focus on some deeper insights into the step “Get Members of Role in Team” from the Workflow Solution Design.

The subsequent process workflow starts by defining an action, which calls all teams of the transmitted project id via API. An empty list will be created to save all the found project members later. After that, the workflow loops through each team of the given project from SAP Cloud ALM and checks if the current team’s name is equal to the team’s name (= “PMO Team”) specified in the parameter section from the Workflow Solution Diagram.

In the affirmative case which highlights that the PMO team was found in the SAP Cloud ALM project, another action is triggered to get all the project roles of this PMO team.

Another for-loop decision traverses through all the roles of the PMO team and checks if the input (= “Requirement Approver”), is found and meets the expected value. Based on this decision, the empty list will be filled with the user’s email address. As soon as the desired project role is found, the for-loop should end the workflow.

Let’s also talk about the termination mechanism. For instance, if the “Requirement Approver” project role wasn’t found in the SAP Cloud ALM team, because it wasn’t either created or no users were maintained in a project role, then several abortions were inserted to prevent an endless loop.

Result

After triggering the request approval status of the requirement in SAP Cloud ALM, the users of the project role “Requirement Approver” will receive an email asking them to review the requirement.

They will get forwarded to the SAP BPA Inbox, where the respective stakeholder can review the and either approve or reject the requirement.

The result of the requirement review process will be updated in SAP Cloud ALM.

Conclusion

For organizations requiring a more customized workflow solution for their requirements in SAP Cloud ALM together with SAP Build Process Automation, my SAP Consulting department offers a consulting service, which can assist in implementing advanced configurations for workflows. Please contact us via the following email to ask about the workflow integration solution: alm.consulting@sap.com.

With these upcoming developments, SAP Cloud ALM is set to become an even more powerful tool for managing workflows and approvals, offering enhanced flexibility and automation capabilities.

entry.title: SAP IBP Configuration - Create a planning level in SAP IBP
entry.content:
In SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP), a planning level defines the granularity at which data is analyzed and planned. It is a crucial component that determines how detailed the planning data will be. Here are some key aspects of planning levels:

  • Attributes: Planning levels are based on attributes derived from master data types, such as product, customer, or location.
  • Granularity: They enable users to plan and analyze data at different levels of detail, from broad categories like product groups to specific details like individual SKUs.
  • Data Storage: Each planning level is defined for a planning area and determines the level at which data is saved.
  • Aggregation: Planning levels allow for data aggregation and disaggregation, facilitating various types of analysis and forecasting.

For example, a planning level might be set at the product and customer level, allowing you to analyze sales forecasts for specific products sold to specific customers.

 

 

Planning Level vs Time Profile

In SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP), planning levels and time profiles serve different purposes but are both essential for effective planning.

Planning Levels

  • Granularity: Planning levels define the granularity of the data, such as product, location, or customer.
  • Attributes: They are based on attributes from master data types and determine the level of detail for planning and analysis.
  • Aggregation: Planning levels allow for data aggregation and disaggregation, enabling detailed or summarized views.

Time Profiles

  • Time Dimensions: Time profiles define the time dimensions used in planning, such as days, weeks, months, and years.
  • Hierarchy: They establish a hierarchy of time buckets, ensuring continuous and non-overlapping periods.
  • Customization: Time profiles can be customized to fit specific planning needs, allowing for different time buckets and periods.

In summary, planning levels focus on the detail and attributes of the data, while time profiles focus on the time dimensions and hierarchy of the planning periods.

1. In the Model Configuration group, choose the Planning Areas Model Configuration tile.

2. Choose your planning area in the list and choose the Planning Levels tab.

3. Choose New, and select the Lowest Time Profile Level, and choose OK.

4. Enter ID and Description.

5. In the Attributes section, choose Add. Select the checkbox to the left of the master data type attributes, and choose Add.

6. Choose Save.

 

Base Planning Level

The base planning level determines on what level planning data will be created.

You need to make sure that the non-time root attributes of the base planning level and the required attributes of the master data type included in the configuration are exactly the same. If you select a base planning level that has only a subset of the attributes, the system may generate inconsistent data.

entry.title: Re: Unlocking Hashmap Magic: Efficient Bulk Data Lookup with Value Mapping in SAP CI using Groovy/XS
entry.content:
Hello @AmanVarshney ,

For XSLT approach, I had to add string() fn on my key variable to make it work.

 Sample XSLT Code:

 

<countryCode>
	<xsl:variable name="key" select="string(countryCode/text())"/>
	<key>
		<xsl:value-of select="$key"/>
	</key>
	<value>
		<xsl:value-of select="map:get($hashmapOutput,$key)" xmlns:map="java:java.util.Map"/>
	</value>
</countryCode>

 

 

 Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Analytic Model was created with older version of the editor
entry.content: I would like to inform you about a regression issue we are currently experiencing. Unfortunately, this regression affects both our development (DEV) and production (PROD) environments. While reporting remains functional, it is no longer possible to modify AMs. This situation complicates things even more for AMs that are currently under development, as they have not yet been documented to allow their recreation. I took the initiative to contact SAP to explore the possibility of desynchronizing their updates between the DEV and PROD tenants. My goal was to avoid any impact on production and to be able to block a regressive update. Unfortunately, SAP's response to this request was negative.

entry.title: Re: Triggering SAP Build Process Automation Processes from SAP Cloud Application Programming (CAP) A
entry.content:
Hi @kun_wu ,

glad to hear that you were able to create the event project.

First, you should check if BPA has returned any errors to your webhook. For that, open your message client, select "Webhooks" and click on "Details" under the actions button. There you should see if there has been any failed delivery. If there was none, it will say "Not applicable".

If there was no issue, you should make sure that you include the correct messageType ("newSuppInvReceived" in your case) as a header parameter in the messaging.emit function.

 

Best regards, Alexander

entry.title: Re: Finding the right CDS Extractor in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi Vipin,

if CDC is missing, it might be for different reasons:
1) Full load is regarded as feasible/accepted, CDC is not required. E.g. for most MD loads.
2) Maybe there is a timestamp/date in the source available, and a generic delta is possible.
Guidance: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/cds-based-data-extraction-part-ii-delta-handling/ba-p/13425761
3) Maybe the source data model is too complex and CDC cannot be provided.
4) SAP might still be working on it
Please check the URL above how to set up your own CDC delta handling.

KR Frank

entry.title: Re: Prevent reprinting the Payment Advice in F110 on S4HC?
entry.content:
Dear MED10,

Thank you for your question. Actually, the PDF icon in your screenshot is used for preview of the PDF file of payment advices, rather than for downloading. The output items section is mainly used for monitoring output request status and relevant parameters.

The printout of payment advices uses the backend printing function. In your screenshot, the status of the first entry for printout is "Completed", which indicates that the PDF is already in the print queue. What you need to do is connect the print queue to your local printer, and print the PDF using the printer. The PDFs in the print queue are only printed once. After the printing, they will be deleted automatically, and will not be printed repeatedly.

For more information, see Before Getting Started | SAP Help Portal.

Kind regards,

Sharon

entry.title: Re: Standard BAPI BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE copy
entry.content:
The item document numbers are returned at the end of the BAPI (after the MB_POST_GOODS_MOVEMENT form has been run).
and so must have been calculated beforehand.

  • As I've always added my ZZ fields at the end of the standard tables, I've never had to solve this problem.
  • You could try to debug BAPI execution, defining a watchpoint on variable XMKPF-MBLNR or a break-point in  FM NUMBER_GET_NEXT ?
  • or you could use some PERFORM ON COMMIT and some assign to XMKPF record 1

entry.title: How to Enable Wildcard Filtering in Search Lists – Cloud Studio SDK
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently working on a development in the Cloud Studio SDK and have encountered a limitation in search lists. While I was able to add a new filter for search functionality, I noticed that it does not support wildcard searches using "*".

For example, instead of requiring users to enter the full value (e.g., "Material001"), we would like them to be able to search using a prefix or suffix with a wildcard (e.g., "MAT"). This would allow the system to return all selections containing the sequence "MAT" within them. However, in the current setup, users must input the exact ID to retrieve a result, making searches less flexible.

#cloudstudio #bydesign

entry.title: Manufacturing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
As SAP continues to refine its cloud offerings, the 2502 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition brings a suite of enhancements specifically tailored for the Manufacturing line of business. This release focuses on enriching features across Production Engineering, Production Planning, Extended Production Planning, Production Operations, and Quality Management. Here's a look of the most important innovations of what's new:

 

Production Engineering 

Process Production Versions: Background Mass Edit

You can now perform background mass edit for the existing production versions. This type of mass edit runs in the background during which you can perform various other actions including mass edits for production versions.
You now have the option to select two types of mass edits:

Foreground

Foreground type does not allow you to perform any actions on the production versions until the mass edit is complete.

Background

Background type allows you to perform actions on the production versions during the mass edit. These actions include create, mass edit, lock, unlock, check, and delete for production versions.

This is the new button for mass edits: 

Process Production Versions - Mass Edit

Here you can see the new mass edit types: 

Process Production Versions - Mass Edit Types

 

Production Planning 

Manage Material Coverage - Planning Time Fence

The stock / requirements list of the Manage Material Coverage (F0251A) app now includes information about the planning time fence or manual firming date of a material.

Manage Material Coverage - Planning Time Fence

You can use a planning time fence to protect procurement proposals from automatic changes during the MRP planning run. If a planning time fence is defined in the material master data or for the MRP group a material is assigned to, the system can automatically firm the following types of receipt elements, depending on the firming type of the material:
• Purchase Requisitions
• Planned Orders
• Delivery Schedule Lines
The same applies to materials with a manual firming date. If the manual firming date is after the end of the planning time fence, the system uses this date to determine which elements are firmed. For materials with a planning time fence or manual firming date, the stock / requirements list in the Manage Material Coverage (F0251A) app now displays a separate row to indicate that a planning time fence or manual firming date is assigned, and to provide information about the corresponding dates. This way, you can easily check which elements have been firmed automatically, because their availability date is before the end of the planning time fence or manual firming date. If you want to adapt the manual firming date for a material, you can also edit it directly from the new row. The changes are immediately reflected in the stock / requirements list.

Capacity Scheduling Board

With this feature, you can now personalize and save a Gantt variant of your app. 

Capacity Scheduling Board - Personalized Variant Settings

This will enable you to save your specific settings for the app every time you want to access it. The settings that can be saved for a personalized variant are the Work Center drop down, Gantt settings, and Work Center settings. These settings are also carried over to the Order Details page and the Manage Operations page. 

Capacity Scheduling Table

With this feature, you can now retain the sequence of displayed orders after a realign operation. This can be done by selecting the Realign Sequence of Orders checkbox in the Realign Schedules window.

Realign Schedules - Retain Sequence of Orders

This functionality is only available when Insert Operation is selected as the Scheduling Control and Future is selected as the Offset. If you are realigning an order with this checkbox selected, to a date where there is already a dispatched order, then the Operation Start for the realigned order will be after the Operation End of the existing dispatched order.

Watch this short demo: 

Capacity Scheduling Table - Demo

 

Mass Maintenance of Products (DD)

With this feature, you can edit Product Specific Parameters on a product level by selecting the Edit button.

Mass Maintenance of Products (DD)

 

You can add these columns to the Product details page by clicking the Settings wheel and selecting the columns. The columns that are added are Product Spike Horizon Constant, Product Spike Horizon Multiplier, and Product Spike Threshold Factor.

 

Replenishment Planning Details

 

Schedule Buffer Proposal Calculation 

With this feature, you can override the default calculation for Buffer Level Zones using the Buffer Level Zone Update (DD_BUFFERLEVELZONE) Business Add-In. 

DD_BUFFERLEVELZONE Business Add-In

Once this Business Add-In (BAdI) is activated, you would be able to view the Custom buffer level zone calculation for products section in Step 3 while creating a proposal in the Schedule Buffer Proposal Calculation app. You can also view further details about the Business Add-In (BAdI) adopted proposals in the Manage Buffer Levels app. Check out this short demo video of how it's done: 

Schedule Buffer Proposal Calculation - Demo

 

Convert Actions in Planned Order (OData V4)

With this feature, two new convert actions, namely Convert to Process Order and Convert to Production Order has been provided with the Planned Order (OData V4) API. 

Planned Order - ODATAV4

 

You can use the above to convert a Planned Order to a Process Order or a Production Order. This feature will not automatically be available in your system but requires that you activate the feature Convert Actions in Planned Order (OData (V4) (S4HC_FTGL_PLND_ORD_RAP_BO) in the configuration activity Activate Features. 

 

Production Execution & Control 

Joule 

​Joule is now generally available and seamlessly integrated in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition after being in early adoption. SAP’s AI copilot Joule leverages generative AI to empower users to execute their everyday tasks more efficiently and use natural language to express their business need.

The Business Objects in Manufacturing that have been enabled with 2502 are the following: 

  • Production Order Confirmation
  • Production Order
  • Production Routing

For further business objects that are on the way, please check this note: 3545050 - List of Business Objects Enabled for Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

For those that want to watch two short teaser demos, here is the one on Production Order: 

Joule Production Order - Demo

 

And here the one for the Production Order Confirmations: 

Joule - Production Order Confirmations - Demo

 

 

Schedule ATP Run for Production/Planned Orders

The Schedule ATP Run for Production/Planned Orders app (F3456) has been enhanced and can now also be used for process orders. Therefore, it has been renamed to Schedule Collective Availability Check for Orders (F3456). 

Schedule Collective Availability Check for Orders

Items Based on ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Editable Co-Products and other Order Features

Enable modification of a production order or process order using a business object interface of the ABAP RESTful application programming model. You can now create, change, or delete co-products as order items. 

Enhanced Production Order Extensibility

Based on ABAP RESTful Programming Model, Post Production Order Confirmations with Goods Movements Using Proposal Data

Enable modification of a production order confirmation as a business object of ABAP RESTful application programming model.

Development Production Order Confirmation Extensibility

OData API Integration of Production Orders: Adding or Removing Serial Numbers

Enhancement of OData APIs for production orders so that you can enable changing production orders by adding or removing serial numbers.

 Enhancement of OData API for production orders for adding or removing serial numbers.

 

Quality Management 

Manage Certificate Receipts

This feature provides the flexibility to manually create certificate receipts within the Manage Certificate Receipts app, enhancing traceability and ensuring quality documentation is in place, even prior to physical goods receipt.

Check out this short demo for more information: 

 

Manage Certificate Receipts - Demo

My colleague Leonie has written a dedicated Quality Management release update blog post for all innovations: Quality Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edi... - SAP Community

If you want to have further details including watching our AI use case in action, please check out this Expert Talk: 


In conclusion, the 2502 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition brings forth powerful enhancements that drive operational efficiencies and augment planning, scheduling, and quality management capabilities. This suite of new features within the Manufacturing line of business demonstrates SAP's commitment to evolving its cloud ERP platform in alignment with industry needs and customer feedback. These tailored tools will undoubtedly empower manufacturers to operate with greater agility and precision in today's dynamic industrial landscape.

entry.title: Slow message processing sometimes - NAST
entry.content:
Hello guys,

our problem is that message processing is sometimes slow.

Most messages are fast proceeded like 0-1 second.
But then there are messages again and again, slow messages with 10 seconds, 1-2 minute, or more.
For example this is delaying printing for minutes.

Time span between ERUHR and UHRVR (table NAST).
Independent from media (print, mail, idoc), sendoption = 4 (direct sending).

In SQL cache the response time for queries to NAST are only milliseconds.
There is also no bottleneck for workprocesses.

do you have any recommendations where to analyze?

Thank you
